meta_title: Impact Windows in Margate, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Margate, FL. Protect your home, lower insurance costs & meet Broward County code. Free estimates!
h1: Impact Windows in Margate, FL — Storm-Smart Protection for Your Home
intro:
Margate homeowners know the drill. Every June, when the Atlantic hurricane season officially opens, the routine begins — checking supplies, watching forecasts, and wondering whether the windows and doors holding your home together are truly up to the challenge. Tucked into the heart of Broward County, Margate sits approximately eight miles inland from the coast, which offers some geographic buffer, but make no mistake: with a designated wind zone of 170 mph, the city is firmly in the crosshairs of powerful tropical systems. Neighborhoods like Oriole Estates, Coral Gate, Margate Estates, and South Margate are filled with hardworking families who have invested significantly in their homes — properties averaging around $310,000 — and those investments deserve real, lasting protection.

The numbers tell a sobering story. Since 1990, the Margate area has experienced nine named storms, and roughly half of all homes in the city were built before 1994, meaning they predate modern Florida Building Code hurricane standards. Older single-family homes in zip codes 33063, 33068, and 33093 were often constructed with single-pane aluminum windows that provide little resistance against wind-driven debris, pressure differentials, or the sustained gusts of a direct hurricane hit. When a storm like Wilma or Irma sweeps through, it's these older homes that suffer the worst consequences — blown-in windows, water intrusion, structural compromise, and costly insurance claims.

Installing impact-resistant windows is no longer a luxury reserved for beachfront estates — it's a practical, financially sound decision for any Margate homeowner. Impact windows from trusted manufacturers like ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI provide a permanent shield against hurricanes, reduce energy bills through Low-E coatings, cut noise pollution from nearby roadways, and can meaningfully lower your annual homeowner's insurance premiums. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, proudly known throughout South Florida as "The Kings of Service," brings over 60 years of combined installation experience directly to Margate — with every project handled in-house, from the first measurement to the final inspection.

section_why.h2: Why Margate Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
Choosing the right impact window company in Margate isn't just about finding the lowest price — it's about finding a team with the skill, accountability, and local knowledge to get the job done right the first time. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties on a simple but powerful foundation: every installation is completed 100% in-house by our own licensed professionals. We do not subcontract your project to third parties. When our crew arrives at your home near Margate Sports Complex or your quiet street in Coral Gate, you're getting the same experienced team that has completed over 500 homes throughout South Florida.

Our 60-plus years of combined team experience means we've seen every type of home configuration that Margate has to offer — from 1970s ranch-style builds in South Margate to newer construction near Coconut Creek's border. We understand the quirks of older aluminum-frame openings, the variance in masonry block construction common throughout Broward County, and exactly what the City of Margate Building Division requires for a clean, code-compliant permit pull. That institutional knowledge saves you time, prevents costly mistakes, and ensures your investment passes inspection without delays.

We also believe that world-class protection shouldn't be financially out of reach. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting as low as $87 per month with zero money down for qualified homeowners, making it genuinely accessible for families across Margate's lower-middle income community. Our A+ BBB Rating and hundreds of verified five-star reviews reflect a commitment to transparent pricing, honest communication, and craftsmanship that stands up to scrutiny — and to storms.

From your initial free estimate through permit coordination with the City of Margate Building Division and final installation, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages every step. We pull the permits, we schedule the inspections, and we stand behind our work with manufacturer-backed warranties and our own service guarantee. When you choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not just buying windows — you're gaining a long-term partner in protecting one of your most valuable assets.

section_history.h2: Margate's Storm History Makes the Case for Impact Windows
section_history.body:
Margate has faced the full fury of South Florida's hurricane history, and the scars left behind by past storms make a compelling argument for permanent, code-compliant storm protection. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 was a watershed moment for the entire region — a Category 5 storm that devastated communities across Broward and Miami-Dade counties and fundamentally reshaped how the state thought about building standards. While Andrew's worst impacts were concentrated to the south, the storm sent a clear message to every Broward County homeowner: no inland location is immune from catastrophic wind damage.

Hurricane Wilma in October 2005 hit particularly close to home for Margate residents. Wilma made landfall on Florida's southwest coast but tracked rapidly northeast, delivering sustained winds of over 100 mph directly through Broward County before anyone had fully processed how quickly conditions had deteriorated. The storm knocked out power to hundreds of thousands of Broward households for weeks and exposed the vulnerability of pre-1994 homes with standard windows and doors. Families near Firefighters Park and throughout the Margate Estates subdivision experienced broken windows, interior flooding, and damaged rooflines that took months to repair.

Hurricane Irma in 2017 reinforced the lesson. Though Irma weakened before reaching Broward County, it still delivered tropical-storm-force gusts that rattled older windows, stripped roofing materials, and sent debris flying through neighborhoods across the city. With nine named storms affecting the area since 1990 and climate scientists warning of more frequent and intense Atlantic hurricane seasons in the decades ahead, the question for Margate homeowners is no longer whether to upgrade — it's how soon.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows in Margate
section_code.body:
Margate falls within Broward County's jurisdiction under the Florida Building Code (FBC), and while the city is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies primarily to Miami-Dade and certain coastal areas — it still falls under stringent hurricane-protection requirements that mandate the use of rated, tested products. All windows and doors installed in Margate must meet the large-missile impact test standard (ASTM E1996) and pressure cycle testing requirements (ASTM E1886) to be considered code-compliant. Products must carry a valid Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or a Florida Product Approval number issued by the Florida Department of Business and Professional Regulation.

For Margate's 170-mph wind zone designation, products must be engineered and tested to withstand those design pressures. This is not a suggestion — it is a legal requirement enforced through the permitting process. Every impact window installation in the city requires a permit pulled through the City of Margate Building Division, a physical inspection of the rough opening and installation, and a final sign-off by a licensed building inspector before the project is considered complete. Homeowners who bypass the permit process risk complications when selling their home, potential denial of insurance claims following storm damage, and the expense of having unpermitted work removed or redone.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the full permit process on behalf of Margate homeowners. Our team prepares the documentation, submits the application to the City of Margate Building Division, coordinates inspection scheduling, and ensures that every product we install carries the appropriate Florida Product Approval. For older homes built before 1994 — which account for approximately half of Margate's housing stock — we also verify that structural openings are properly reinforced to accept modern impact products, ensuring both code compliance and maximum performance in a storm event.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: Premium Products Built for Broward County
section_products.body:
At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we install two industry-leading brands that are purpose-built for South Florida's demanding climate: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both manufacturers produce impact-resistant window systems that combine laminated glass with high-performance multi-chamber frame designs, delivering outstanding protection against the 170-mph wind loads specified for Margate's wind zone — all while maintaining the clean, modern aesthetics that today's homeowners expect.

ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) has earned a strong reputation across Broward County for the quality and durability of its aluminum and vinyl impact window lines. Their products feature heavy-duty laminated glass that consists of two panes of tempered glass bonded with a resilient interlayer — so even if the outer pane cracks under impact, the interlayer holds the glass in place and the window maintains its structural integrity. This is critical during a storm, because it's the breach of a window opening — not just the initial impact — that allows catastrophic pressure changes to develop inside a home. ESW windows also incorporate Low-E (low-emissivity) coatings that reflect heat-generating infrared light, helping Margate homeowners reduce solar heat gain and lower cooling costs throughout the brutal South Florida summer.

CWI products complement our ESW lineup with a range of impact window systems designed for versatility across different home styles and budget tiers. Their multi-chamber frame construction improves thermal efficiency and structural rigidity, while their product approval certifications ensure they meet all Florida Building Code requirements for Broward County installations. Whether you're outfitting a 1970s ranch home in Oriole Estates or a newer build in South Margate, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's team will assess your specific openings and recommend the right ESW or CWI solution to deliver maximum protection, energy savings, and curb appeal.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Windows Cost in Margate — And How to Finance Them
section_cost.body:
One of the most common questions we hear from Margate homeowners is: how much will this actually cost? The honest answer depends on variables like the number of openings, window sizes, frame material, glass package, and the condition of existing openings in your home. For a typical Margate single-family home — a three-bedroom, two-bath property in the $310,000 range — a full impact window replacement project generally falls in the range of $8,000 to $20,000, depending on the scope of work. Larger homes or those with picture windows, custom shapes, or significant structural prep work may fall toward the higher end of that range.

For a city where many residents are working-class families building equity in their first homes, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has designed a financing program that makes this investment genuinely attainable. Qualified homeowners can get started with $0 down and monthly payments beginning at just $87 per month. When you factor in the real financial returns — reduced energy bills, insurance premium savings (often hundreds of dollars per year), and the documented increase in resale value that impact windows add to a South Florida home — the project frequently pays for itself within a few years and continues delivering returns long after.

It's also worth considering the cost of not upgrading. A single hurricane event that breaches a standard window can cause tens of thousands of dollars in interior water damage, mold remediation, and personal property loss — expenses that insurance may not fully cover, particularly as carriers continue tightening their coverage terms in Florida. Investing in ESW or CWI impact windows through Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is ultimately a form of financial self-protection, securing the value of your Margate home for years to come.

section_insurance.h2: Lower Your Homeowner's Insurance with Impact Windows in Margate
section_insurance.body:
Florida's homeowner's insurance market is one of the most expensive in the nation, and Broward County residents know this reality all too well. Premiums have climbed sharply over the past decade as carriers absorb losses from repeated storm seasons. The good news for Margate homeowners is that installing code-compliant impact windows can trigger meaningful insurance discounts — reductions that, in many cases, substantially offset the cost of the upgrade over time.

The mechanism behind these savings is the wind mitigation inspection. After impact windows are installed and permitted, homeowners can request a wind mitigation inspection from a licensed inspector, who documents the storm-protection features of the home on a standardized form submitted to your insurance carrier. Impact-rated windows and doors are one of the most heavily weighted factors in this form, because they directly reduce a carrier's risk exposure for wind and water intrusion claims. Depending on your current policy and carrier — whether that's Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, or another Broward County-active insurer — verified impact window installations can reduce your annual wind premium by 15% to 45%.

On a Margate home paying $4,000 to $6,000 per year in homeowner's insurance — a common range in today's market — a 25% wind mitigation discount translates to $1,000 to $1,500 in annual savings. Over ten years, that's $10,000 to $15,000 back in your pocket, which can entirely cover the cost of your window installation. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides all the documentation you'll need to pursue your wind mitigation discount, and we're happy to walk you through the process of filing with your carrier to make sure you capture every dollar of savings you've earned.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact windows in Margate if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Margate is located in a 170-mph wind zone under the Florida Building Code, and the city has been directly affected by multiple hurricanes including Wilma and Irma. Being eight miles inland does not significantly reduce wind speeds during a major storm. Approximately half of Margate's homes predate modern hurricane standards and are particularly vulnerable to wind-driven debris and pressure failure. Impact windows provide critical protection for any home in the area, regardless of coastal proximity.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost for a typical Margate home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: For a standard single-family home in Margate — typically three to four bedrooms averaging around $310,000 in value — a full impact window replacement project generally ranges from $8,000 to $20,000 depending on the number of openings, window sizes, and any structural preparation required. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down for qualified homeowners, making it accessible for Margate families across all income levels. Contact us for a free, no-obligation estimate tailored to your specific home.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to install impact windows in Margate, and does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle that?
faqs.a: Yes, a building permit is required for all impact window installations in Margate, processed through the City of Margate Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — from application submission and documentation preparation to inspection scheduling and final sign-off. All products we install carry valid Florida Product Approval numbers and meet Broward County's wind zone requirements. You won't need to navigate any paperwork on your own.

faqs.q: How long does a typical impact window installation take in Margate?
faqs.a: For most Margate single-family homes, the physical installation of impact windows is completed in one to two days by our in-house crew. The overall project timeline, including permit approval from the City of Margate Building Division, typically runs two to four weeks from contract signing to final inspection. Our team coordinates every step, so you'll know exactly what to expect and when. We do not subcontract — the same experienced Premier Impact Windows & Roofing professionals who measure your openings complete the full installation.

faqs.q: Will impact windows actually lower my homeowner's insurance in Broward County?
faqs.a: Yes, in most cases significantly. Once your impact windows are installed and permitted, you can request a wind mitigation inspection. The results are submitted to your insurance carrier, and verified impact-rated openings are one of the most heavily weighted credits on the wind mitigation form. Broward County homeowners commonly see wind premium reductions of 15% to 45%. On typical Margate insurance premiums, that can translate to $1,000 or more in annual savings — making impact windows one of the highest-ROI home improvements you can make.
